title: Great Pyramid of Giza (Khufu)
description: The oldest and largest of the three pyramids in Giza, built for Pharaoh Khufu (Cheops). 4th Dynasty, Old Kingdom.

title: Pyramid of Khafre
description: The second-largest pyramid at Giza, retaining some of its original limestone casing at the apex. 4th Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Menkaure
description: The smallest of the three main Giza pyramids, notable for its granite lower courses. 4th Dynasty.

title: Great Sphinx of Giza
description: A limestone statue of a reclining sphinx, commonly believed to represent Pharaoh Khafre. Old Kingdom.

title: Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM)
description: The largest archaeological museum in the world, overlooking the Giza Pyramids. Modern era, housing Tutankhamun's full collection.

title: Tomb of Meresankh III
description: A beautifully decorated mastaba tomb of the Queen Meresankh III, featuring rock-cut statues. Giza East Field.

title: Tomb of Idu
description: A hidden gem in the Eastern Cemetery of Giza, belonging to the Scribe of the Royal Documents. 6th Dynasty.

title: Tomb of Qar
description: Locally known as the Tomb of Meryre-nefer, located next to Idu's tomb. Features an open court. 6th Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Djedefre
description: Located in Abu Roash, this ruined pyramid was built on a high hill, making it originally look taller than Giza's. 4th Dynasty.

title: Layer Pyramid
description: A ruined step pyramid located in Zawyet el-Aryan, attributed to Pharaoh Khaba. 3rd Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Sahure
description: The first pyramid built in the Abusir necropolis, known for its finely carved reliefs. 5th Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Neferirkare
description: The largest pyramid at Abusir, originally built as a step pyramid and later cased. 5th Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Niuserre
description: Located between Sahure and Neferirkare pyramids in Abusir. 5th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba of Ptahshepses
description: One of the largest and most complex non-royal tombs from the Old Kingdom, located in Abusir. 5th Dynasty.

title: Sun Temple of Userkaf
description: Ruins of a sun temple in Abusir, distinct from pyramids, dedicated to Ra. 5th Dynasty.

title: Step Pyramid of Djoser
description: The oldest colossal stone building in Egypt, located in the Saqqara necropolis. 3rd Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Unas
description: Small pyramid in Saqqara famous for being the first to contain the Pyramid Texts. 5th Dynasty.

title: Pyramid of Teti
description: From the outside a ruin, but inside contains well-preserved Pyramid Texts. Saqqara. 6th Dynasty.

title: Serapeum of Saqqara
description: A massive underground gallery containing enormous granite sarcophagi for the Apis Bulls. Hidden gem.

title: Mastaba of Ti
description: One of the most beautifully decorated tombs in Saqqara, belonging to a high official. 5th Dynasty.

title: Mastaba of Mereruka
description: The largest individual tomb in Saqqara with 33 rooms, located near Teti's pyramid. 6th Dynasty.

title: Tomb of Horemheb (Unfinished)
description: Located in the New Kingdom necropolis of Saqqara, built before he became Pharaoh. 18th Dynasty.

title: Tomb of Maya
description: The tomb of Tutankhamun's treasurer, featuring beautiful reliefs. Saqqara New Kingdom cemetery.

title: Pyramid of Sekhemkhet
description: The "Buried Pyramid", an unfinished step pyramid attributed to Djoser's successor. Saqqara.

title: Imhotep Museum
description: Modern museum at the foot of the Saqqara necropolis displaying objects found in the area.

title: The Philosophers Circle
description: A hemicycle of statues of Greek poets and philosophers found at the Serapeum way.

title: Mastaba of Kagemni
description: Tomb of a Vizier with refined reliefs of birds and daily life. Located near Teti's pyramid, Saqqara.

title: Pyramid of Userkaf
description: Located close to the Djoser complex, now largely in ruins. 5th Dynasty.

title: Tomb of Niankhkhnum and Khnumhotep
description: The "Two Brothers" tomb, famous for its intimate depictions of the two manicurists. Saqqara.

title: Bubasteion (Cat Mummies)
description: Temple complex dedicated to Bastet, containing thousands of cat mummies. Saqqara cliffs.

title: Memphis Open Air Museum
description: Museum in Mit Rahina displaying the Colossus of Ramses II and other artifacts from the ancient capital.

title: Colossus of Ramses II
description: A massive limestone statue of Pharaoh Ramses II, housed lying down in the Memphis museum building.

title: Alabaster Sphinx of Memphis
description: A large sphinx carved from a single block of calcite alabaster. Mit Rahina.

title: Bent Pyramid
description: A unique pyramid in Dahshur with a change in angle, representing a transition in pyramid design. Sneferu.

title: Red Pyramid
description: The first successful smooth-sided pyramid, built by Sneferu. Dahshur.

title: Black Pyramid
description: The ruined mudbrick pyramid of Amenemhat III in Dahshur. Middle Kingdom.

title: Pyramid of Senusret III
description: Built of mudbrick, now a mound, located in Dahshur. Known for the jewelry of the princesses found nearby.

title: Valley of the Golden Mummies
description: A massive burial site in Bahariya Oasis containing many mummies with gilded masks. Greco-Roman.

title: Tomb of Bannentiu
description: A well-preserved tomb in Qarat Qasr Salim, Bahariya Oasis. 26th Dynasty.

title: Temple of Alexander the Great
description: One of the few temples dedicated to Alexander in Egypt, located near Qasr el-Megisbah, Bahariya.

title: Temple of Ain el-Muftella
description: Four chapels built during the 26th Dynasty under Amasis. Bahariya Oasis (El Bawiti).

title: Gebel al-Ingleez (English Mountain)
description: A hill in Bahariya with World War I lookout ruins, offering panoramic views of the oasis.

title: Tomb of Zed-Amun-Ef-Ankh
description: Burial of a wealthy merchant/official found in Bahariya's Qarat Qasr Salim. 26th Dynasty.

title: Orman Botanical Garden
description: Historic botanical garden in Giza establishing in 1875 by Khedive Ismail.

title: Giza Zoo
description: The oldest zoological garden in Egypt and the Middle East, opened in 1891.

title: Ahmed Shawki Museum
description: The home of the "Prince of Poets" Ahmed Shawki, converted into a museum continuously on the Giza Corniche.

title: Wissa Wassef Art Centre
description: An architectural and cultural landmark in Harraniya, Giza, famous for traditional tapestries.

title: Southern Tomb
description: An enigma of the Djoser complex, featuring faience tiles similar to the Step Pyramid interior. Saqqara.
